items.title: 'Life is football' here but as a favourite son heads home, it needs help

children.content: The Tiwi Islands are unique and Australian Rules football is a way of life that has spawned some of the game's biggest names. But even as favourite son Willie Rioli Jr returns home, the Tiwi Bombers know they need more help.

gallery.altText: A man sits on the grass shirtless next to other footy players. Some other men are standing in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A man sits on the grass shirtless next to other footy players. Some other men are standing in the background.

items.title: 'Beautiful, kind, caring man' dies in hospital months after alleged bashing

children.content: Friends of Darwin man Martin Wiberg, 48, are remembering him as a "very gentle soul" after he died in hospital this week.

image.altText: Man wearing sunglasses through a hole in a cement wall

items.title: Top End tourism and hospitality businesses seek lifeline amid rising costs

children.content: A popular Darwin restaurant has closed down and the owner of another local hospitality business is asking for COVID-era support measures to be brought back as costs rise and visitor numbers fall.

image.altText: The front door of a suburban restaurant building, padlocked closed.

items.title: Diphtheria response criticised for not being fast enough

children.content: Rapid vaccination could stem the spread of the potentially deadly diphtheria outbreak, but the government's response has been criticised as being too slow.

articles.title: NT health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across the outback

children.content: As diphtheria spreads across four different Australian jurisdictions in the largest outbreak since records began, public health messaging in the NT — the region most impacted by the serious disease — has been hard to come by.

image.altText: A woman holding up her t-shirt sleeve, revealing a small band-aid over the spot where she was vaccinated.

articles.title: Health officials warn cases of diphtheria could rise

children.content: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

image.altText: A doctor with his arms folded stands in front of a window

articles.title: Outbreak of highly contagious infection grows to more than 220 cases

children.content: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

gallery.altText: Dr Casey smiles into the camera.

thumbnail.altText: Dr Casey smiles into the camera.

items.title: Man arrested after allegedly raping elderly woman in her own home

children.content: A man who police allege raped an elderly woman in her own home on Wednesday night before fleeing the scene has been arrested.

image.altText: The bonnent of an NT police car that reads "police" and has the NT police force emblem. Green leaves blurred in right corner.

items.title: From two-way radio to satellite, School of the Air celebrates 75 years

children.content: The world's oldest school of the air is celebrating its 75th anniversary in Alice Springs. It provides a service to families over 1.3 million square kilometres and also is known as the world's largest classroom.

image.altText: A young girl speaking into an old radio in the 1950s.

items.title: Former NT political staffer claims unfair dismissal, airs bullying allegations

children.content: A former staffer for Northern Territory government backbencher Clinton Howe is claiming she was sacked for refusing to falsify constituent information in a database, and was bullied and micromanaged by the politician's full-time electorate officer.

image.altText: A large photo of a smiling politician fills the front window of an office building, the front door reads: Clinton Howe

items.title: Parents of children in foster care say 'black eyes' and bite marks were ignored

children.content: The parents of two children in Northern Territory foster care have spoken out about their "heartbreaking" experience with the system, saying concerns their daughter was being abused by her foster family were brushed off by the department.

image.altText: A woman in a sleeveless, long dress, long brown hair in kitchen, blurred background, with brown teddy bear focus in foreground.

items.title: Entrepreneur says the rich should stop complaining about tax changes

children.content: Start-up founders say the federal government's CGT changes may see investment in new businesses go offshore. But Airtree Ventures co-founder Daniel Petre is warning against a scare campaign.

gallery.altText: Daniel is older, Caucasian man, grey hair, looks seriously, sits in front of the computer, wears thick black-rimmed glasses.

thumbnail.altText: Daniel is older, Caucasian man, grey hair, looks seriously, sits in front of the computer, wears thick black-rimmed glasses.

items.title: Why there's more talk of an AUKUS 'plan B'

children.content: Defence industry experts argue Australia should consider how to prevent a naval "capability gap" — or risk being left exposed.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: A new battleground has started for Israel, where words trump actions

children.content: In the wake of an outcry over its treatment of detained activists, Israel has focused on messaging as its actions undermine its support from the international community.

image.altText: Benjamin Netanyahu looking stern at a press conference.

items.title: Nine charts that reveal who pays more under Labor's tax changes

children.content: Labor's capital gains tax changes have set off a firestorm of debate about the implications for small businesses, start-ups, share owners and plenty more. Here's who will really pay more.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers

items.title: The budget's central weakness was revealed this week

children.content: As debate over the federal government's budget tax hit on wealth and assets continues to reverberate, one can't help but wonder how it's going down among the gen Zs and millennials it's designed to impress.

image.altText: Men in suits speak at a press conference

items.title: How the Musk v Altman case revealed an 'uncomfortable' truth

children.content: After Sam Altman and OpenAI's recent victory in a drawn-out legal battle with billionaire Elon Musk, the bitter rivalry between the former friends is now set to move from the courtroom to Wall Street.

gallery.altText: Elon Musk sits on chair on stage next to Sam Altman and an interviewer.

thumbnail.altText: Elon Musk sits on chair on stage next to Sam Altman and an interviewer.

items.title: Children's entertainer Danny Go!'s son dies of cancer aged 14

children.content: The popular children's YouTuber revealed the news after documenting the illness since it was diagnosed late last year. Tributes have flowed from Children's entertainers, including the Wiggles.

image.altText: The YouTuber shared the news of his son's diagnosis in December, last year.

items.title: ASIO holiday warning did not flag elevated Hanukkah threat

children.content: Australia's intelligence agency, ASIO, issued its annual holiday threat assessment less than two weeks before the Hanukkah terrorist attack at Bondi Beach but did not identify heightened risks to the Jewish festival, despite earlier declaring antisemitism as its top priority in terms of threat to life.

image.altText: People hug at floral tribute Bondi Beach promenade

items.title: The mission to save conjoined twin involved an airlift, five surgeons and a heartbreaking sacrifice

children.content: A team of doctors involved in the separation of conjoined Papua New Guinean twins Sawong and Tom have spoken for the first time about the rare and complex case.

gallery.altText: A close up of a Papua New Guinean baby smiling from a cot.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a Papua New Guinean baby smiling from a cot.
Conjoined twins lie together on a small cot.

items.title: 'There's more in the tank:' Pendlebury celebrates games record despite Eagles scare

children.content: Collingwood's No.10 enjoys a 10-point win in his record-breaking match as the Magpies hold firm to down the Eagles at the MCG.

image.altText: An AFL player in black and white stripes is carried off the field on the shoulders of two others. Camera flash to the left

items.title: Brent Harvey is about to lose his place in AFL history

children.content: Brent 'Boomer' Harvey has enjoyed being the AFL/VFL games record holder for the past decade, and says he could have played more if he'd accepted an offer from other clubs to extend his career.

image.altText: Brent Harvey smiles while sitting on an armchair. He wears a black t-shirt.

items.title: Giant iceberg with shipping containers spotted off Antarctica

children.content: Details of how the shipping containers ended up on a giant iceberg have been revealed in one of the more unusual reports tabled at the annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations.

gallery.altText: A large rectangular iceberg with seven dark dots on it, seen from a satellite photo.
Shipping containers sitting on a massive iceberg.
A research station on pylons in Antarctica.

thumbnail.altText: A large rectangular iceberg with seven dark dots on it, seen from a satellite photo.
Shipping containers sitting on a massive iceberg.

items.title: Police investigate triple fatal crash in Sydney's south

children.content: Three people have died in a car crash in Sydney's south, the ABC understands one of the vehicles was likely a rideshare car.

image.altText: Police and fire cars parked near a crashed car.

items.title: The prickly plant powering a new spirits industry

children.content: Australia's emerging agave industry is attracting interest from distillers, farmers and scientists who believe the spiky plant could transform everything from tequila production to sustainable aviation fuel.

image.altText: Close up of an agave plant.

items.title: Suzie Miller took the risky path away from a safe job as a judge, with no regrets

children.content: In 2009, Suzie Miller had a choice between becoming a magistrate in Australia or a residency at the National Theatre in London.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of Prince, Twin Peaks and some very important donkeys

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX launches upgraded Starship

children.content: Elon Musk's rocket company has launched its new Starship design to enable more frequent Starlink satellite launches and to send future NASA missions to the moon.

image.altText: People looking over the ocean watching a rocket take off

items.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump's director of national ​intelligence

children.content: Tulsi Gabbard says she is departing from the role to support her husband after his recent bone cancer diagnosis.

image.altText: A woman wearing white speaks into a microphone as she looks diagonally to her right

items.title: Is Australia's two-party political system dead?

children.content: With the rise of One Nation's popularity and younger voters increasingly turning to the Greens, the traditional two-party system in Australian politics has been dismantled, a former political strategist argues.

image.altText: Composite of Greens volunteers holding placards and Pauline Hanson meeting One Nation voters

items.title: The perfect warm winter salad using crisp-roasted artichokes and grains

children.content: Crispy artichokes and textural grain make this salad a satisfying dinner and excellent next-day lunch.

image.altText: A grey serving bowl of salad, with browned artichoke hearts on top, sits on a bench.

items.title: When the bushfire came, the Walkers turned on their roof sprinkler and left

children.content: Preparing for an ember attack could extend your home's bushfire survivability rate to above 90 per cent or even closer to 100 per cent, a fire scientist says.

gallery.altText: Aerial perspective of a coastal home spinkler system on roof surrounded by burned trees under a blue sky.
